name: Iron Mountain Incorporated

profile.description: Iron Mountain is a global leader in information management services trusted by 240,000+ customers in 61 countries, including 95% of the Fortune 1000. The company operates physical records storage, data center colocation, digital transformation, and asset lifecycle management businesses, and has been organized as a REIT since 2014.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Iron Mountain has successfully transformed from a physical records storage company into a hybrid data center and digital services platform. FY2025 revenue hit a record $6.9B, with data center, digital, and ALM units growing over 40% in Q4 2025 [1]. The company has 400MW of new data center capacity coming online in the next 24 months [1]. AI Deployment (1/2): Won the 2025 Mass Tech Leadership Council top award for AI Implementation, deploying AI across digital solutions for document classification and intelligent records management [2]. Market Share (1/2): Iron Mountain is the dominant player in physical records storage globally with 95% Fortune 1000 penetration, though data center market share remains modest versus hyperscalers [1]. Pipeline (1/2): 400MW data center pipeline with 25%+ data center revenue growth expected in 2026 and 20%+ in 2027, plus continued ALM expansion [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Significant investment in digital transformation and AI capabilities relative to REIT peers [2].

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Iron Mountain's purpose centers on 'unlocking value and intelligence from stored assets' and commitment to safety, security, and sustainability. CEO William Meaney has led a distinctive transformation from paper storage to digital services, with the 'mates take care of mates' culture forming an identifiable organizational ethos [2]. Community Impact (2/2): Iron Mountain has been on the FTSE4Good Index for 8 consecutive years, scored 100 on the Human Rights Campaign workplace equity scorecard, joined RE100 for renewable energy, and committed to Science Based Targets for carbon reduction [3]. Employee Retention (1/2): The executive team is rated A+ by Comparably (Top 5% for companies with 10,000+ employees), and the 'mates' culture is referenced as a differentiator, though specific turnover metrics are not publicly disclosed [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO Meaney has maintained significant equity exposure throughout the transformation, aligning leadership incentives with the data center pivot strategy [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Revenue growth of 16.6% YoY is exceptional for a REIT, driven by the data center and digital segments [1]. Forward P/E of 46.1x is elevated but reflects high-growth data center exposure with 2026 guidance of $7.6-7.8B revenue (12% growth) and AFFO/share of $5.69-$5.79 [1]. Profit margin of 2.1% is optically low due to REIT depreciation distortions — AFFO-based metrics show healthy cash generation. Dividend has been paid for 17+ consecutive years with ~6% 5-year CAGR, growing from $0.618/quarter in 2021 to $0.864 currently [4]. Payout ratio on GAAP earnings is meaningless for a REIT; on AFFO it is sustainable. Liquidity floor easily cleared at $204M daily traded value [4].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Iron Mountain's physical records storage business is inherently immune to AI disruption — AI cannot replace the need to store, retrieve, and securely destroy physical documents under regulatory mandates. More importantly, the data center expansion directly benefits from AI demand; the company's 400MW pipeline is being built precisely to serve AI workloads [1]. AI creates more demand for Iron Mountain's infrastructure, not less. Digital transformation services (document digitization, intelligent classification) become more valuable as enterprises adopt AI and need their unstructured data made machine-readable. AI Opportunity (1/1): Iron Mountain won the 2025 Mass Tech Leadership Council award for AI Implementation and is deploying AI-powered products across its digital solutions platform, generating measurable revenue from AI-enhanced document management and data classification services [2].

infinite_game.summary: Under CEO William Meaney, Iron Mountain has demonstrated remarkable existential flexibility — transforming from a paper storage company into a data center and digital services platform. The company's willingness to cannibalize its legacy identity in pursuit of long-term relevance is classic infinite game behavior. However, the pace of transformation creates execution risk, and the competitive language around data center market capture has finite overtones.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Iron Mountain's purpose of 'protecting and unlocking value from stored information' has genuine depth and has guided the company from physical vaults to digital transformation to AI-ready data centers [1]. The cause is distinctive and has shaped strategic decisions — but it hasn't yet been tested through a leadership transition or major crisis under the current vision. The pivot itself demonstrates purpose-driven evolution.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): The 'mates take care of mates' culture and A+ executive team rating from Comparably (Top 5%) suggest above-average organizational trust [2]. The 29,400-employee workforce spans 61 countries, and the company has maintained cultural coherence through a massive strategic transformation. However, no specific retention or turnover data to confirm exceptional trust levels.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Iron Mountain competes against hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) in data centers and acknowledges their scale advantage while differentiating on colocation flexibility and compliance expertise [1]. Management frames competition as motivation to improve its hybrid physical-digital value proposition rather than a zero-sum battle for dominance.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): Iron Mountain's transformation from a physical records storage company into a data center and digital services platform is a textbook example of existential flexibility [1]. The company invested billions in data center capacity while its legacy business was still profitable, deliberately cannibalizing its identity as a 'box storage' company. Data center and digital revenue now drive growth, with 400MW of new capacity scheduled, fundamentally redefining what Iron Mountain is.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): CEO Meaney's decision to invest aggressively in data centers — committing billions in capex while the physical storage business was stable and profitable — required real courage [1]. The 2026 guidance of $7.6-7.8B revenue with double-digit EBITDA growth validates the bet. However, the transformation has been market-validated for several years now, reducing the ongoing 'courage cost' of the strategy.

investment_thesis: Iron Mountain is a rare REIT successfully pivoting from legacy physical storage into high-growth data center infrastructure, riding the AI-driven demand wave for colocation capacity. FY2025 revenue hit a record $6.9B with data center and digital units growing 40%+ in Q4, and 2026 guidance of $7.6-7.8B (12% growth) with AFFO/share of $5.69-$5.79 significantly beat consensus [1].
thesis_continued: The 400MW data center pipeline positions Iron Mountain to capture AI-driven demand through 2027+, while the legacy physical storage business provides highly recurring revenue and 95% Fortune 1000 penetration [1]. The dividend yield of 2.93% with 17+ years of consecutive payments and ~6% 5-year growth rate provides income quality. The primary risk is execution on the capital-intensive data center buildout in a competitive market, with elevated leverage and a forward P/E of 46x requiring sustained growth to justify the valuation.
key_catalysts: 400MW of new data center capacity coming online over the next 24 months, with 25%+ data center revenue growth expected in 2026 [1]
AFFO/share guidance of $5.69-$5.79 for 2026 significantly above consensus estimate of $4.22, suggesting analyst upgrades ahead [1]
AI-driven enterprise demand for data digitization and intelligent document management creating cross-sell opportunities across 240,000+ customer base [2]
key_risks: Elevated valuation at 46x forward P/E requires sustained double-digit growth; any data center demand slowdown could compress multiples significantly
Capital-intensive data center buildout with unknown D/E ratio creates balance sheet risk — REIT structure limits retained earnings for organic funding
Competition from hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, Google) with deeper pockets and larger data center footprints could limit market share gains in colocation
sources: Iron Mountain Q4/FY2025 Earnings Release and 2026 Guidance, February 2026
Comparably Executive Team Ratings and Mass Tech Leadership Council AI Implementation Award, 2025
FTSE4Good Index Inclusion and Human Rights Campaign Scorecard, accessed April 2026
Yahoo Finance — IRM key statistics and dividend history, accessed April 2026
